title: Cleanup base: merge by distance + fill holes + recalc normals
when_to_use: Primo cleanup post-import per la maggior parte degli STL AI. Risolve buchi piccoli (loops <= 8 lati), vertici doppi sotto soglia, normali inconsistenti. Triggers in routing_rules.yaml: R001 (dissolve degenerate), R004 (non-manifold + holes).
preconditions: no modifiers blocking edit mode (apply them first if needed)

merge_distance_mm.description: Distance threshold in MILLIMETERS for vertex merge. 0.001mm = 1 micron, safe default that won't damage geometry. Increase to 0.01 for very dense AI meshes.

fill_max_sides.description: Max polygon sides for fill_holes. Loops larger than this stay open (use voxel remesh fallback).

steps.label: Select target and enter edit mode
steps.code:
import bpy
for o in bpy.context.selected_objects:
    o.select_set(False)
obj = bpy.data.objects['{object_name}']
obj.select_set(True)
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = obj
bpy.ops.object.mode_set(mode='EDIT')
bpy.ops.mesh.select_all(action='SELECT')
print('Entered edit mode on', obj.name)

steps.label: Merge by distance (remove duplicate vertices)
steps.code:
import bpy
# {merge_distance_mm} is in millimetres. Blender units = metres by default,
# so convert: 0.001 mm = 1e-6 m. If scene.unit_settings.scale_length=0.001
# (1 BU = 1mm) you can pass merge_distance_mm directly.
scale = bpy.context.scene.unit_settings.scale_length
threshold = ({merge_distance_mm} * 0.001) / scale
bpy.ops.mesh.remove_doubles(threshold=threshold)
print('remove_doubles threshold=', threshold)

steps.label: Exit edit mode
steps.code:
import bpy
bpy.ops.object.mode_set(mode='OBJECT')
print('Back to object mode')

verification.notes: Re-run analyze_mesh_for_print and compare to the input that triggered the routing rule. All three repair metrics should be non-increasing; normals should be 'consistent' if the mesh is now watertight.
